methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Rapidus, a late-stage semiconductor company founded by Atsuyoshi Koike, has raised $5.0B+ to date. The semiconductor sector includes 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, though narrow-stage peer multiples are unavailable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the private set.

Valuation triangulation relies on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using total capital raised ($5.0B) as a proxy for current value and applying a standard 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $6.0B. Public semiconductor comparables (P/S median 24.2x) exist but cannot be applied given the absence of disclosed revenue for the target. Peer-Median analysis across the 14 same-sector private companies fails the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints after filtering for stage and valuation tier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, centered on $6.0B. This range reflects stage-based progression from the raised capital base; however, confidence is constrained by reliance on a single method and the absence of comparable private transactions or public revenue anchors. Key uncertainties include actual capital deployment efficiency relative to similar-stage peers, proximity to revenue inflection, and the timing and terms of any intermediate financing rounds.
